question_title: What will be the value of "Crude Oil Prices: Brent - Europe" on 2026-02-13?
summary: Brent crude (DCOILBRENTEU) closed at $67.72 on Feb 2, 2026, after a sharp -6.3% single-day drop from $72.25 (Jan 30). The drop appears driven by US-Iran negotiation progress reducing risk premiums. Over the 9 trading day horizon to Feb 13, price movement is dominated by random-walk dynamics. Monte Carlo simulations using daily volatility of ~2.0-2.2% (slightly elevated to reflect recent turbulence) consistently produce a median around $67.5-67.7. The bearish analyst consensus for 2026 ($55/bbl average) and oversupply narrative provide mild downside bias, but 9 days is too short for structural trends to dominate. Key risks include further US-Iran de-escalation (bearish), tariff escalation (direction uncertain), and OPEC+ decisions.

factors.description: Current FRED Brent spot price of $67.72 on Feb 2 anchors the forecast median
Sharp -6.3% drop on Feb 2 from US-Iran talks reducing risk premium - suggests possible continued downside pressure
January 2026 rally from ~$61 to $72 could partially resume if geopolitical tensions re-escalate
Bearish longer-term analyst consensus ($55/bbl avg 2026 from ABN AMRO), oversupply narrative
Short 9-day horizon means random walk dominates; limited scope for trend or mean reversion
Elevated recent volatility (3.0% daily last 10 days vs 2.0% full sample) widens uncertainty band

reasoning:

**Key reasoning:**

- 9 trading days is a short horizon where random-walk dynamics dominate
- Monte Carlo simulation using ~2.2% daily volatility (slightly elevated from 2.0% full-sample to reflect recent turbulence) centers the distribution near $67.7
- Mild bearish bias from US-Iran de-escalation and oversupply consensus, partially offset by potential for geopolitical re-escalation
- 80% confidence interval: roughly $64–$72
sources_consulted: [Brent Crude Oil Last Day Financ (BZ=F) Stock Historical Prices & Data - Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/BZ=F/history/)
